class.name: RopeAnchor
class.brief_description: An anchor point on a [Rope] that can optionally apply tension forces to a parent [RigidBody3D].
class.description: [RopeAnchor] is a [Node3D] that serves as an attachment point along a [Rope]. Place it in the scene and assign its [NodePath] to one of the rope's anchor slots to make that section of rope follow the node's transform. When parented to a [RigidBody3D], the [constant TOWING] and [constant SLIDING] behaviors enable the rope to apply tension forces back to the body using [method apply_force]. Subclasses can override [method get_particle_count] and [method get_particle_position] to provide multiple positions from a single anchor node, for example to represent a coiled drum or a pulley with multiple contact points. Emits [signal anchor_changed] whenever a property that affects the anchor's geometry or behavior changes.

method.name: apply_force
method.description: Applies [param force] to the parent [RigidBody3D] at this anchor's global position. The offset from the body's origin is computed automatically. Has no effect if the parent is not a [RigidBody3D].

method.name: get_parent_rigid_body
method.description: Returns the parent node if it is a [RigidBody3D], otherwise returns [code]null[/code].

method.name: get_particle_count
method.description: Returns the number of anchor positions this node provides. The base implementation always returns [code]1[/code]. Override in a subclass to supply multiple positions (e.g. for a coiled rope section). The rope uses the returned count to call [method get_particle_position] for each index.

method.name: get_particle_position
method.description: Returns the local-space position of the anchor at index [param idx]. The base implementation returns [code]Vector3(0, 0, 0)[/code] (the node's local origin). Override in a subclass to return a different position per index.

members.member: Controls how the rope interacts with this anchor. See [enum AnchorBehavior] for available modes.
Friction coefficient for this anchor. [code]0.0[/code] allows the rope to slide freely; [code]1.0[/code] makes it stick. Only used when [member behavior] is [constant GUIDED] or [constant SLIDING].

signal.name: anchor_changed
signal.description: Emitted whenever a property that affects the anchor's geometry or behavior changes, such as [member behavior] or [member friction].

constants.constant: The rope is unattached to this anchor. The anchor is ignored by the rope simulation.
The rope is fixed to this anchor and cannot slide through it. The anchor does not move.
The rope is fixed to this anchor and pulls on its parent [RigidBody3D] via tension forces. Acts like [constant ANCHORED] if the parent is not a [RigidBody3D].
The rope slides freely through this anchor (like a fairlead or ring). The anchor does not move, but the rope's speed through the point is controlled by [member friction].
The rope slides through this anchor and also pulls on its parent [RigidBody3D] at the contact point. Combines [constant GUIDED] sliding behavior with [constant TOWING] force feedback.
